county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lane County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is Dighton. Named for James H. Lane, US Senator and Free State leader. Western Kansas agricultural county. Sparsely populated. Dust Bowl effects. Several failed town sites.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lane County Register of Deeds in Dighton holds land records from the 1880s. A sparsely populated western Kansas county. The Lane County Historical Society and Museum in Dighton has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Methodist and Catholic congregations. Dighton Herald newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration. The county had several failed town sites whose records may be at KSHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Lane County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Dighton

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Leavenworth County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Leavenworth. Named for Colonel Henry Leavenworth, founder of Fort Leavenworth (1827). One of the oldest settlements in Kansas. Fort Leavenworth is the oldest active US Army post west of the Mississippi. US Penitentiary at Leavenworth (1903). Major Missouri River port and railroad center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Leavenworth County Register of Deeds holds land records from 1855. Leavenworth is one of Kansas's oldest cities and was a major Missouri River port and military supply center. Fort Leavenworth (established 1827) military records at NARA are extensive and predate Kansas Territory. The US Penitentiary at Leavenworth (1903) has prisoner records at NARA. The Leavenworth County Historical Society and Museum has excellent local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Strong German, Irish, and African American communities left diverse church records. Probate Court records from 1855. Leavenworth Times newspaper at KSHS (from 1857). FamilySearch has microfilmed Leavenworth County records. District Court naturalization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Leavenworth County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Leavenworth

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lincoln County formed 1867, organized 1870. County seat is Lincoln Center. Named for President Abraham Lincoln. Post Rock Limestone country. Danish, Swedish, and German immigrant communities. Saline River valley settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lincoln County Register of Deeds in Lincoln Center holds land records from the 1870s. The Lincoln County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Danish, Swedish, and German immigrant communities left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Lincoln Sentinel newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Lincoln County records. The Saline River valley was the primary settlement corridor. Post Rock Limestone area -- quarry and construction records may document workers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Lincoln County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Lincoln Center

county_metadata.record_losses: Marais des Cygnes massacre (1858) and border war raids destroyed early settlement records; some courthouse records lost
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Linn County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Mound City. Named for Lewis F. Linn, US Senator from Missouri. Severely affected by border war violence. Marais des Cygnes massacre (1858). John Brown operated here. Eastern Kansas border county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Linn County suffered significant record losses from the Marais des Cygnes massacre (1858) and border war raids that destroyed early settlement records. Register of Deeds in Mound City holds surviving and post-war records. KSHS in Topeka holds territorial-era documents. BLM GLO land patents fill gaps in destroyed land records. The Linn County Historical Society and Museum in Pleasanton has local collections. The Marais des Cygnes Massacre State Historic Site has related documentation. John Brown operated in Linn County; check KSHS manuscript collections. Probate Court records may have gaps in earliest years. Mound City and Pleasanton newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed surviving Linn County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Linn County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Mound City

source_profiles.source_name: Linn County Land Records (post-loss) -- Register of Deeds / BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Linn County Register of Deeds, Mound City / BLM GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County land records with gaps due to Marais des Cygnes massacre (1858) and border war raids destroyed early settlement records; some courthouse records lost. BLM GLO federal land patents supplement destroyed county deed books. Register of Deeds holds surviving and post-loss records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Incomplete: Marais des Cygnes massacre (1858) and border war raids destroyed early settlement records; some courthouse records lost. BLM GLO patents provide partial backup for federal land entries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Logan County formed 1881 from Wallace County. County seat is Oakley. Named for General John A. Logan, Civil War officer. Western Kansas county along the Union Pacific Railroad. Dust Bowl effects. Sparsely populated agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Logan County Register of Deeds in Oakley holds land records from the 1880s. Formed from Wallace County -- check Wallace County records for earlier entries. The Logan County Historical Society and Fick Fossil Museum in Oakley has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations. Oakley newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Union Pacific Railroad records at KSHS may document workers and land sales. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration in the 1930s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Logan County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Oakley

county_metadata.parent_county: Original (unorganized territory -- originally Breckinridge County, renamed 1862)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lyon County formed 1855 as Breckinridge County (renamed Lyon 1862). County seat is Emporia. Named for General Nathaniel Lyon, first Union general killed in Civil War. Emporia State University. William Allen White's Emporia Gazette. Welsh settlers. Flint Hills region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lyon County (originally Breckinridge County until 1862) Register of Deeds in Emporia holds land records from 1855. Emporia State University archives hold local history materials. The Lyon County Historical Society and Museum in Emporia has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. William Allen White's Emporia Gazette (from 1895) at KSHS is a nationally significant newspaper. Church records from Methodist, Presbyterian, and Catholic congregations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. FamilySearch has microfilmed Lyon County records. When searching early records, use 'Breckinridge County' for the pre-1862 period. The county attracted Welsh settlers who left distinct church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Lyon County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Emporia

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marion County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Marion. Named for General Francis Marion, 'Swamp Fox' of the American Revolution. Major Mennonite settlement area (Hillsboro, Goessel). German-Russian Mennonite immigrants brought Turkey Red wheat to Kansas. Tabor College in Hillsboro.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marion County Register of Deeds in Marion holds land records from 1855. Major Mennonite settlement area -- Hillsboro and Goessel communities have extensive Mennonite church records. The Marion County Historical Society and Museum in Marion has local collections. Tabor College (Mennonite Brethren) archives in Hillsboro hold church records. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian Mennonite immigrants brought Turkey Red wheat to Kansas from the 1870s. Probate Court records from 1855. Marion newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Marion County records. The Mennonite Heritage and Agricultural Museum in Goessel documents immigration patterns. Peabody was a significant early town.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Marion County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Marion

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marshall County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Marysville. Named for Frank J. Marshall, operator of a ferry on the Big Blue River. Oregon Trail, California Trail, and Pony Express route. Marysville was a major trail-era outfitting point.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marshall County Register of Deeds in Marysville holds land records from 1855. Marysville was a major stop on the Oregon Trail, California Trail, and Pony Express route. The Marshall County Historical Society and Museum in Marysville has local collections including trail-era records. BLM GLO land patents. German Catholic, Irish, and Scandinavian immigrant communities left church records from the 1850s-1870s. Probate Court records from 1855. Marysville newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Marshall County records. The Pony Express Station museum has related documentation. District Court naturalization records at the courthouse document immigrant arrivals.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Marshall County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Marysville

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: McPherson County formed 1867, organized 1870. County seat is McPherson. Named for General James B. McPherson, killed in Civil War. Major Mennonite and Swedish settlement area. Bethany College (Swedish Lutheran) in Lindsborg. McPherson College (Church of the Brethren).
county_metadata.research_tips: McPherson County Register of Deeds in McPherson holds land records from 1870. The county was a major Mennonite and Swedish settlement area. McPherson College (Church of the Brethren) and Central Christian College archives hold church-related records. The McPherson County Old Mill Museum and Historical Society has excellent local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Swedish Lutheran church records (Lindsborg area, Bethany College) and Mennonite church records are particularly important. Probate Court records from 1870. McPherson newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed McPherson County records. The Birger Sandzen Memorial Gallery in Lindsborg documents Swedish heritage. Bethany College archives hold Swedish immigrant files.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for McPherson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in McPherson

source_profiles.source_name: McPherson County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: McPherson County Probate Court, McPherson / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for McPherson County from 1867; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Meade County formed 1885 from Ford County. County seat is Meade. Named for General George G. Meade, Civil War commander. Dalton Gang hideout. Southwestern Kansas ranching and farming county. Dust Bowl effects.
county_metadata.research_tips: Meade County Register of Deeds in Meade holds land records from 1885. Formed from Ford County -- check Ford County records for earlier entries. The Meade County Historical Society and Dalton Gang Hideout in Meade has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from 1885. Church records from pioneer Methodist and Baptist congregations. Meade Globe-Press newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county experienced Dust Bowl effects. Cattle ranching records at KSHS may document early families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Meade County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Meade

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Meade County Register of Deeds, Meade
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Meade County from 1885; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1885s-1895s

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Meade County Probate Court, Meade
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Meade County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1890-1950 for Meade County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Meade County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Meade County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Meade County Historic Newspapers -- KSHS
source_profiles.repository: Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Meade County towns at KSHS on microfilm; legal notices, homestead proof notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, and community news; essential for documenting early settlement in western Kansas
